ci() uses execFileSync with argument array (no shell), plus null byte guard added
dist/ pre-flight check already present at install() lines 62-65
Error-code-specific handling already present: ENOENT, EACCES/EPERM, ENOSPC, generic fallback at lines 74-84

Fixed checksum double-serialize: save() now spreads the state object directly instead of re-parsing JSON, eliminating the fragile parse-serialize-parse-serialize round-trip. Checksum is computed over canonical JSON of state, then state+checksum is serialized once for the file.
Already fixed in prior commit (D02-fix comment on line 78). backupCurrentState() is already inside the withLock() body, making backup+write atomic under the lock.
Fixed lock file handle leak: wrapped handle.writeFile+close in try/finally in both acquireLock paths (loop body and force-remove retry). Handle is now always closed even if writeFile throws.

Fixed Windows PID check: isLockStale() now uses 30s timestamp threshold as PRIMARY check (stale if age >= 30s). PID liveness check (process.kill(pid,0)) is only used as SECONDARY on non-Windows (process.platform \!== 'win32'). On Windows, only timestamp is trusted.

Fixed infinite crash loop: stateStore.save(failed) in inject-context.ts error boundary is now wrapped in nested try/catch. If save throws, the error is silently caught and the failure prompt message is still returned to the user.
Added isAbsoluteOrUnsafePath() guard to reject Windows absolute paths (backslash, drive-letter) in file_exists gate predicate
Narrowed envOpt type from confusing union to { env?: Record<string,string> } and removed dead envPart guard
Fixed parseEnv regex to fallback-match when env: is last section (no flow:/done when: following)
Already implemented: findSimilarPredicate() imported and used in evaluate-completion.ts with fuzzy matching, tests verified
Replaced separate and/or search with findRightmostOperator() for true left-to-right associativity — a and b or c is now (a and b) or c
Added goal sanitization (collapse newlines to spaces) and spawn name validation (alphanumeric+hyphens only, throws on invalid)

Enhanced PreCompact hook: now uses renderFlowCompact()+renderFlowSummary() for token-efficient compaction context, adds awaiting_capture detection with capture retry prompt re-injection, includes debug logging. Test added for awaiting_capture scenario. All 56 hook tests pass.

Added compaction-aware re-injection to SessionStart hook. When state has awaiting_capture nodeProgress, re-emits buildCaptureRetryPrompt() in additionalContext so variable capture survives session resume after compaction. findAwaitingCapture()+findNodeById() helpers added. Integration test verifies capture prompt with nonce is included in stdout JSON.
renderNodeToDsl now escapes newlines to spaces in prompt/run text

Added debug/verbose mode. Created src/presentation/hooks/debug.ts with debug() helper checking PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG env var. Writes to stderr with [PL:debug] prefix. Added debug logging to session-start.ts (state load, cleanup, capture re-injection), user-prompt-submit.ts (state transitions, prompt modification, completion), and pre-compact.ts (flow preservation, capture detection). 4 unit tests for debug helper (env on/off/0/false). Set PROMPT_LANGUAGE_DEBUG=1 to enable.
Changed timeoutSeconds != null to !== undefined (0 no longer falsy)
Removed node:crypto from domain — nonce uses pure JS Math.random, createSessionState accepts optional nonce param
AutoAdvanceResult refactored to tagged union with kind: prompt|advance|pause
Foreach re-entry uses cached maxIterations from NodeProgress
Nonce increased from 32-bit to 128-bit (4 segments of 8 hex chars)

MCP research findings: (1) No existing MCP config (.mcp.json or tool_server) in the project — plugin uses hooks-only architecture via hooks.json. (2) An MCP tool like flow_capture_response(varName, value) would be more deterministic than XML tag capture since it bypasses text parsing entirely. (3) Benefits: eliminates tag spoofing risk (no nonce needed), removes capture retry loop, simplifies PostToolUse tag scanning. (4) Complexity: requires MCP server implementation (stdio transport), Claude Code plugin MCP support (still evolving), and backwards-compat shim for older Claude Code versions without MCP. (5) Risk: MCP plugin support is not yet stable in Claude Code — hooks API is the supported integration point. (6) Recommendation: defer MCP capture to a future milestone when Claude Code plugin MCP support stabilizes. Keep tag+file dual capture as current strategy. The nonce anti-spoofing (H-SEC-004) adequately mitigates tag injection.
